templateMeta.name: Blog Growth Template
templateMeta.description: Autonomous blog growth with content creation, distribution, analytics, and site branding

mission.description: One-sentence blog purpose: What is this blog for? Who does it serve?

strategy.description: Freeform content strategy. Include: topics/pillars, target audience, voice/tone, competitors to cover, special content angles.

sources.description: URLs, GitHub repos, podcasts, communities to monitor for content ideas

referrals.description: Referral/affiliate links and guidance on when to use them

telegramTopicId.description: Telegram topic/thread ID (optional, for forum groups)

liveBlogUrl.description: The live blog URL where content is published

invariants.condition: You ensure all content aligns with mission: {{mission}}
invariants.assessment: For each published post, answer: 'What specific aspect of {{mission}} does this serve?' If any post has no clear answer, condition fails.

do: Ask 'Does this serve our mission?' before publishing
Prioritize content that directly advances the mission
dont: Chase trends that don't align with mission

invariants.condition: You operate as CEO: reading {{strategy}}, creating NEW invariants from strategy elements, and delegating to managers with relevant parent invariants
invariants.assessment: Each GOAL area has a dedicated manager. Each manager's blueprint includes: (1) NEW invariants derived from {{strategy}} relevant to their scope, (2) relevant parent invariants from this blueprint. Zero direct GOAL execution by this job.

do: Analyze {{strategy}} to identify content pillars, target audience, voice, distribution channels
Create NEW invariants from strategy (e.g., 'CONTENT-PILLAR-AI: You focus on AI/ML topics' derived from strategy)
Include relevant parent invariants (e.g., Content Manager gets GOAL-CONTENT, GOAL-SOURCES-*)
Dispatch Content Manager with content-relevant strategy invariants
Dispatch Distribution Manager with channel strategy invariants
Dispatch Site Manager with branding guidance invariants
dont: Pass entire {{strategy}} blob as text to managers
Omit relevant parent invariants from manager blueprints
Write blog posts, edit code, or post to social directly
Give managers scope beyond their GOAL area

invariants.condition: You ensure managers delegate their work to specialists rather than executing directly
invariants.assessment: Manager jobs dispatch specialist workers for execution. Managers coordinate and review, they do not write content, edit code, or post directly.

do: Content Manager dispatches Writer jobs for each post
Site Manager dispatches Developer jobs for code changes
Distribution Manager dispatches Promoter jobs for each channel
Managers review and approve specialist output before completion
dont: Manager posts to social media directly
Skip delegation and execute GOAL work at manager level

invariants.assessment: Count posts per cycle that cite or reference at least one URL from {{sources}}

do: Regularly check source URLs for updates and trends
Use sources as starting points for broader research

invariants.assessment: Count posts per cycle that cite sources discovered through google_web_search or web_fetch (not in {{sources}})

do: Research emerging trends not in provided sources
Find primary sources and data to support claims
dont: Only use provided sources without exploration

invariants.condition: You include referral links only in contextually relevant content with proper disclosure
invariants.assessment: For each referral link: (1) surrounding content genuinely relates to the product, (2) affiliate disclosure present

do: Include referral when genuinely recommending a relevant product
Disclose affiliate relationships transparently
dont: Shoehorn referral links into unrelated content

invariants.assessment: Rate each post 0-100 using: Originality (25%) - unique insights not found elsewhere; Depth (25%) - thorough coverage with supporting evidence; Actionability (25%) - reader can apply learnings; Audience fit (25%) - matches target audience from strategy. Average across posts.

do: Original research with data
Step-by-step tutorials solving real problems
Case studies with measurable results

invariants.assessment: Before writing, use blog_list_posts to review existing content. Score 0-100 using: Topic Freshness (25%) - does the post cover a topic/angle not recently explored? Deduct points for substantial overlap with posts from past 30 days; Format Variety (25%) - does the content type (tutorial, analysis, news, deep-dive, interview) differ from the last 3 posts? Deduct if same format repeated; Additive Value (25%) - if covering a similar theme, does it build upon rather than repeat previous posts? Full points for new insights, zero for rehash; Catalog Awareness (25%) - evidence that existing posts were reviewed before planning? Full points if post explicitly references or differentiates from existing content.

do: Use blog_list_posts before planning any new content
Rotate content formats: tutorial → analysis → news → deep-dive
Explicitly note 'This builds on our previous post about X by exploring Y'
Track topics covered in past month to identify gaps
Write complementary pieces that link to each other
dont: Write multiple 'intro to AI agents' posts with different titles
Repeat the same conclusions across posts
Ignore the existing content catalog when planning
Use the same format (e.g., listicle) for consecutive posts
Cover identical news from the same angle twice

invariants.condition: You EXECUTE distribution by broadcasting to Telegram (write-only) and planning platform-agnostic strategies.
invariants.assessment: Verify that: (1) At least one Telegram message is sent using `telegram_send_message` (or similar) per cycle if new content exists or for general engagement. (2) Strategy does NOT assume X/Reddit/LinkedIn access. (3) Strategy does NOT assume reading Telegram.

do: Call telegram_send_message to share latest post
Broadcast updates to Telegram subscribers
Optimize for SEO and organic discovery
Pursue backlinks from relevant sites
Create embeddable content others can share
Build email list for direct distribution
dont: Only create a plan without executing it
Plan X/Twitter thread campaigns (no account access)
Assume any platform login we don't have

invariants.condition: You report analytics insights to enable data-driven decisions
invariants.assessment: Analytics workstream produces artifact containing: (1) key metrics (visitors, pageviews, top pages), (2) trend direction (up/down/flat), (3) one actionable recommendation. Root job consumes this for cycle summary.

do: Create analytics artifact with metrics and trends
Identify top-performing content and hypothesize why
Recommend content adjustments based on data

invariants.assessment: Compare 7-day rolling average of daily visitors to prior 7-day period. Value = ((current - prior) / prior) * 100. Min 0 = no decline.

do: Track daily visitor trends
Identify and respond to traffic spikes/drops

invariants.assessment: Compare unique visitors this month vs last month. Value = ((this - last) / last) * 100. Target 5% monthly growth.

do: Set monthly growth targets
Adjust strategy if growth stalls
dont: Chase vanity metrics over real growth

invariants.assessment: The blog is hosted at {{domain}}. Rate site 0-100 using: Mission clarity (25%) - does landing page communicate {{mission}} within 5 seconds?; Visual identity (25%) - do colors, typography, imagery reinforce the blog's tone?; Navigation (25%) - can visitors easily find content aligned with their interests?; Differentiation (25%) - is the site distinct from template defaults and competitors?

do: Design choices that reinforce the mission
Clear value proposition above the fold
Visual identity consistent with content tone
Distinctive branding that stands out

invariants.condition: You ensure all content changes pass build validation
invariants.assessment: After content changes, build completes successfully with exit code 0

do: Run build after content changes
When build fails with MDX errors, search mdxjs.com for syntax help
Use google_web_search to research unfamiliar MDX patterns
dont: Assume Markdown syntax works in MDX without verification

invariants.condition: You gate all deployable work through review before merge
invariants.assessment: No content or code reaches production without explicit approval. Rejections include specific feedback for improvement.

do: Review work quality before approving
Reject substandard work with actionable feedback
Only merge work meeting quality standards
dont: Merge work that doesn't meet GOAL-CONTENT standards

invariants.condition: You produce a cycle summary capturing metrics, learnings, and strategy adjustments
invariants.assessment: Cycle ends with summary artifact containing: (1) metrics vs targets, (2) what content performed best and why, (3) specific adjustments for next cycle

do: Synthesize analytics reports from managers
Document successful experiments
Adjust manager priorities based on data
